attacking_style: Early, repeated accelerations to establish solo leads on climbs, but lacks sustained power to hold off fresh chasers. Typically exhausts capacity in single major effort without recovery for subsequent climbs.
team_tactics: Limited domestique support evident — relies on individual efforts rather than team-controlled tempo. TTT performances suggest weak team infrastructure (Astana 2018).
terrain_preference: Time trials on familiar roads show genuine strength. Struggles on multi-climb stages requiring recovery between efforts. Single sustained climbs better than successive climbing tests.
vulnerability: Cannot recover after major breakaway efforts — fades badly on subsequent climbs same day. Vulnerable when caught by fresher riders from chase groups who haven't spent energy earlier.
key_patterns: Multiple early accelerations to gain solo position, but fades when caught (2017, 2019)
Strong time trial performances, especially on home Swiss roads (2018 Romandie benchmark)
Single-effort climber without capacity for sustained multi-climb racing (2019 drifted back post-Oberalp)
Actual GC form masked by poor team support in TTTs (2018 Astana cost 55s)
form_trajectory: negative: 2019 showed declining recovery capacity and single-effort limitation, drifting backwards after breakaway attempts unlike earlier competitive TT and climbing performances
